PERSONALITY.name: Openness
PERSONALITY.for_students: Openness means how curious you are about new ideas, experiences, and learning new things. Students high in openness enjoy creativity, imagination, and exploring different perspectives.
PERSONALITY.for_professionals: Openness reflects an individual's willingness to embrace new ideas, adapt to change, think innovatively, and explore unconventional solutions in professional settings.

PERSONALITY.name: Agreeableness
PERSONALITY.for_students: Agreeableness shows how kind, cooperative, and understanding you are with others. Students high in agreeableness are supportive, friendly, and good team players.
PERSONALITY.for_professionals: Agreeableness represents the ability to collaborate effectively, demonstrate empathy, maintain harmonious relationships, and work cooperatively within teams.

PERSONALITY.name: Conscientiousness
PERSONALITY.for_students: Conscientiousness means how responsible, organized, and disciplined you are. Students high in this trait complete assignments on time and stay focused on goals.
PERSONALITY.for_professionals: Conscientiousness indicates reliability, strong work ethic, goal orientation, time management, and the ability to maintain consistency in performance.

PERSONALITY.name: Extraversion
PERSONALITY.for_students: Extraversion shows how energetic and social you are. Students high in extraversion enjoy group activities, speaking up, and interacting with others.
PERSONALITY.for_professionals: Extraversion reflects confidence, assertiveness, enthusiasm, and comfort in networking, leadership roles, and client-facing situations.

PERSONALITY.name: Neuroticism
PERSONALITY.for_students: Neuroticism measures how emotionally sensitive or reactive you are. Students with higher levels may feel stress or worry more easily.
PERSONALITY.for_professionals: Neuroticism indicates emotional reactivity and stress sensitivity. Lower levels are associated with emotional stability, resilience, and calm decision-making under pressure.

ORIENTATION.name: Creative Orientation
ORIENTATION.for_students: Creative orientation means you enjoy imagination, designing, storytelling, art, or thinking differently.
ORIENTATION.for_professionals: Creative orientation reflects innovation-driven thinking, ideation capability, and the ability to develop original concepts and solutions.

ORIENTATION.name: Practical Orientation
ORIENTATION.for_students: Practical orientation means you like real-world tasks, hands-on work, and solving everyday problems.
ORIENTATION.for_professionals: Practical orientation indicates a results-focused mindset, preference for implementation, and efficiency in executing tangible solutions.

ORIENTATION.name: Social Orientation
ORIENTATION.for_students: Social orientation means you enjoy helping, teaching, or interacting with people.
ORIENTATION.for_professionals: Social orientation reflects strong interpersonal engagement, people-centered motivation, and preference for collaborative environments.

ORIENTATION.name: Analytical Orientation
ORIENTATION.for_students: Analytical orientation means you enjoy thinking deeply, solving puzzles, and understanding how things work.
ORIENTATION.for_professionals: Analytical orientation represents structured thinking, data-driven decision-making, problem-solving ability, and logical evaluation skills.

APTITUDE.name: Logical Aptitude
APTITUDE.for_students: Logical aptitude is your ability to think clearly, find patterns, and solve reasoning problems.
APTITUDE.for_professionals: Logical aptitude reflects structured reasoning, systematic analysis, and the ability to derive conclusions through critical thinking.

APTITUDE.name: Spatial Aptitude
APTITUDE.for_students: Spatial aptitude is your ability to imagine shapes, designs, and how objects fit together.
APTITUDE.for_professionals: Spatial aptitude indicates visualization capability, design interpretation skills, and proficiency in understanding structures, layouts, and models.

APTITUDE.name: Verbal Aptitude
APTITUDE.for_students: Verbal aptitude is your ability to understand language, express ideas clearly, and communicate effectively.
APTITUDE.for_professionals: Verbal aptitude reflects strong communication skills, comprehension ability, articulation, and clarity in written and spoken expression.

APTITUDE.name: Numerical Aptitude
APTITUDE.for_students: Numerical aptitude is your ability to work with numbers, calculations, and data.
APTITUDE.for_professionals: Numerical aptitude represents quantitative reasoning, data interpretation, financial analysis, and accuracy in numerical problem-solving.

MOTIVATION.name: Achievement Motivation
MOTIVATION.for_students: Achievement motivation means you like setting goals and working hard to succeed.
MOTIVATION.for_professionals: Achievement motivation reflects goal orientation, performance drive, competitiveness, and the pursuit of excellence.

MOTIVATION.name: Leadership Motivation
MOTIVATION.for_students: Leadership motivation means you like guiding others and taking responsibility in group tasks.
MOTIVATION.for_professionals: Leadership motivation indicates a desire to influence, direct teams, take ownership, and drive organizational outcomes.

MOTIVATION.name: Helping Motivation
MOTIVATION.for_students: Helping motivation means you feel happy when you support or assist others.
MOTIVATION.for_professionals: Helping motivation reflects service orientation, mentoring tendencies, empathy-driven engagement, and commitment to others' growth.

MOTIVATION.name: Creativity Motivation
MOTIVATION.for_students: Creativity motivation means you enjoy making new ideas, designs, or solutions.
MOTIVATION.for_professionals: Creativity motivation indicates intrinsic drive toward innovation, experimentation, and generating novel approaches in work settings.

MOTIVATION.name: Security Motivation
MOTIVATION.for_students: Security motivation means you prefer stability, clear plans, and safe choices.
MOTIVATION.for_professionals: Security motivation reflects preference for structured environments, job stability, predictable systems, and long-term security.
